Analise de Doenças Cardíacas

Exploração do dataset de Doenças Cardíacas

Dataset vem desse link

Carregando Bibliotecas

Code 
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

from ucimlrepo import fetch_ucirepo 

from sklearn import tree
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.preprocessing import MinMaxScaler
from imblearn.over_sampling import RandomOverSampler
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

Carregando Dataset

Code 
# fetch dataset 
heart_disease = fetch_ucirepo(id=45) 
df = heart_disease.data.features.copy()

assert isinstance(df, pd.DataFrame)

df['target'] = heart_disease.data.targets['num']

Observando Dataset

Nota-se valores numéricos com graus distintos.

Code 
df.head(10)

Entendendo features

Code 
df = df.sort_values(by='target', ignore_index=True)

df.plot(
    y=list(df.columns),
    secondary_y=['target']
)

Analisando valores nulos

Não existem muitos valores nulos.

Code 
df[df.isna().any(axis=1)]
age sex cp trestbps chol fbs restecg thalach exang oldpeak slope ca thal target
0 58 1 2 125 220 0 0 144 0 0.4 2 NaN 7.0 0
2 38 1 3 138 175 0 0 173 0 0.0 1 NaN 3.0 0
51 53 0 3 128 216 0 2 115 0 0.0 1 0.0 NaN 0
111 52 1 3 138 223 0 0 169 0 0.0 1 NaN 3.0 0
208 43 1 4 132 247 1 2 143 1 0.1 2 NaN 7.0 1
237 52 1 4 128 204 1 0 156 1 1.0 2 0.0 NaN 2
Code 
print(f"relação 'nan/total': {(len(df[df.isna().any(axis=1)])/len(df)):0.2f}")
relação 'nan/total': 0.02

Ou seja, faz sentido excluí-los.

Code 
sns.heatmap(df.isna())
Code 
quantidade_nulos = df.isna().sum()
df = df.dropna()

Analisando distribuição de classes

As classes são bem desiguais, o que torna um estudo preciso difícil.

Code 
sns.countplot(x='target', data=df)

Verificando duplicatas

Como não há nenhum campo que sirva como identificador, vamos presumir que a granularidade está definida por pessoas diferentes.

Analisando matriz de correlação

Code 
df.columns.tolist()
['age',
 'sex',

Code 
plt.matshow(df.corr())
plt.show()

Explorando outros gráficos

Gráfico de histograma:

Code 
#sns.pairplot(df, hue="target")
df.hist(figsize=(12, 8), bins=20)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Gráfico de disperção:

Code 
sns.stripplot(data=df.drop(columns=["target"]), orient="h")
plt.show()
Code 
sns.stripplot(data=df[["age", "trestbps", "chol", "thalach", "oldpeak"]], orient="h")
plt.show()
Code 
sns.stripplot(data=df.drop(columns=["age", "trestbps", "chol", "thalach", "oldpeak"]), orient="h")
plt.show()

Gráfico box-plot:

Code 
sns.boxplot(data=df)
plt.show()

Gráfico de violino:

Code 
sns.violinplot(data=df[["age", "trestbps", "chol"]])
plt.show()

Preparando dataset para ML

Isso inclui normalização dos datasets usando min-max para por os valores entre 0 e 1 e oversampling para corrigir desbalanço de classes.

Code 
scaler = MinMaxScaler()

y = df['target']
x = df.drop(columns=['target'])

x_train, x_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
    x,
    y,
    stratify=y,
    test_size=0.2,
    random_state=42
)

x_train = scaler.fit_transform(x_train)
x_test = scaler.transform(x_test)

ros = RandomOverSampler(random_state=42)

x_train, y_train = ros.fit_resample(x_train, y_train)

print(y_train.value_counts())
target
1    128
0    128
3    128
4    128
2    128
Name: count, dtype: int64

Explorando árvores de decisão

Code 
clf = tree.DecisionTreeClassifier()
model = clf.fit(x_train, y_train)

tree.plot_tree(
    model,
    filled=True,
    feature_names=x.columns,
    class_names=True,
    max_depth=1
) 

prediction = model.predict(x_test)
print(classification_report(y_test, prediction))
              precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.67      0.75      0.71        32
           1       0.00      0.00      0.00        11
           2       0.20      0.14      0.17         7
           3       0.17      0.14      0.15         7
           4       0.00      0.00      0.00         3

    accuracy                           0.43        60
   macro avg       0.21      0.21      0.21        60
weighted avg       0.40      0.43      0.41        60

Explorando K-Means

Code 
inertia = []

k_range = range(1, 11)

for k in k_range:
    model = Pipeline(
        steps=[
            ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
            ("kmeans", KMeans(
                n_clusters=k,
                random_state=42,
                n_init="auto"
            ))
        ]
    )

    model.fit(x)

    inertia.append(
        model.named_steps["kmeans"].inertia_
    )

plt.plot(k_range, inertia, marker='o')
plt.xlabel("Número de clusters")
plt.ylabel("Inertia")
plt.title("Método do Cotovelo")
plt.show()
Code 
final_model = Pipeline(
    steps=[
        ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
        ("kmeans", KMeans(
            n_clusters=3,
            random_state=42,
            n_init="auto"
        ))
    ]
)

final_model.fit(x)

scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_x = scaler.fit_transform(x)

pca = PCA(n_components=2)
components = pca.fit_transform(scaled_x)

clusters = final_model.named_steps["kmeans"].labels_

df["cluster"] = clusters
df.head()

df.groupby("cluster").mean(numeric_only=True)

kmeans = final_model.named_steps["kmeans"]

centroids = pca.transform(
    kmeans.cluster_centers_
)

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))

plt.scatter(
    components[:, 0],
    components[:, 1],
    c=clusters
)

plt.scatter(
    centroids[:, 0],
    centroids[:, 1],
    s=300,
    marker="X"
)

plt.xlabel("PC1")
plt.ylabel("PC2")
plt.title("KMeans Clusters with Centroids")

plt.show()
